content: I just got back from an amazing week in Thailand! The street food in Bangkok was incredible - I've been trying to recreate some dishes at home.
Thailand sounds amazing! What was your favorite street food dish that you're trying to recreate?
Definitely the pad thai from this little cart near Khao San Road. I'm a real estate agent in Austin, so I had to take some time off to make this trip work.
That's quite a journey from Austin to Bangkok! How long have you been a real estate agent?
About three years now. I actually started right after college. The job gives me flexibility for travel, which is perfect since I'm really into travel blogging.
That's a great combination - real estate and travel blogging. Do you focus on any particular type of real estate in Austin?
Mostly residential, but I've been getting into some urban development projects lately. I'm also really into urban sketching, so I bring my sketchbook everywhere I go.
Urban sketching sounds fascinating! Do you share your sketches on your travel blog?
Yeah, I post them along with my writing. I've been trying to improve my painting skills too. Oh, and I'm taking care of my aging parents in Denver while working remotely from Austin.
That's a lot to balance - work, travel, art, and family care. How do you manage it all?
It's definitely a juggling act. I try to visit them in Denver every few months. My dad used to collect vintage cars, so I've inherited that interest too.
Vintage cars are such a cool hobby! Do you have a favorite model or era?
I'm really into 1960s muscle cars. I've been working on restoring a '67 Mustang for the past year. It's been a great stress reliever from work.
A '67 Mustang sounds amazing! That must be quite the project. How's the Thai cooking coming along?
Getting better! I found some authentic ingredients at this Asian market in Austin. The key is getting the balance of sweet, sour, salty, and spicy just right.
That's the secret to great Thai food! Have you thought about your next travel destination?
I'm thinking about Vietnam next. I want to explore the food scene in Hanoi and Ho Chi Minh City. Plus, I could use some beach time in between work commitments.
Vietnam would be incredible for food and beaches! Since you're a real estate agent, do you notice any interesting architectural differences when you travel?
Absolutely! Thailand's mix of modern and traditional architecture was fascinating. I'm always looking at how different cultures approach space and design.
That's such a unique perspective to bring to your travels. Any must-try spots in Vietnam you've already researched?

I've heard amazing things about the street food markets in Hanoi's Old Quarter. And I definitely want to check out some of the French colonial architecture there. Oh, and I need to make sure my parents are settled before I go!
Sounds like you've got a great plan shaping up! The French colonial architecture in Hanoi is stunning. Safe travels whenever you decide to go!
